channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: The Open LIVE! Detry leads with MacIntyre, Fitzpatrick chasing
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football

item.title: Next UK PM urged to end Labour Party’s ‘boys club’
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
As Andy Burnham prepares to be UK’s next PM, he faces calls to ensure women get half of top jobs in his cabinet and end the Labour Party’s ‘boys club’.
Read More: https://punchng.com/next-uk-pm-urged-to-end-labour-partys-boys-club/

item.title: Tinubu intentionally rebuilding South-East, says Soludo
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Anambra Governor Soludo praises President Tinubu’s commitment to rebuilding the South-East through critical infrastructure projects, including two major fe
Read More: https://punchng.com/tinubu-intentionally-rebuilding-south-east-says-soludo/

item.title: World Cup: English FA backs Tuchel despite semi-final loss
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The English FA reaffirms support for head coach Thomas Tuchel after England’s World Cup semi-final defeat, backing him through Euro 2028. Read more about T
Read More: https://punchng.com/world-cup-english-fa-backs-tuchel-despite-semi-final-loss/

item.title: World Cup: Argentina face FIFA sanction over Falklands banner display
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Argentina could face FIFA sanction after players displayed a Falklands banner following their World Cup semi-final win. Learn more about the political mess
Read More: https://punchng.com/world-cup-argentina-face-fifa-sanction-over-falklands-banner-display/

item.title: Fear persists, Oyo teacher laments lack of security after pupils’ rescue
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Despite rescued pupils, an Oyo teacher laments lingering fear due to lack of visible security. Learn how this impacts school attendance and staff morale.
Read More: https://punchng.com/fear-persists-oyo-teacher-laments-lack-of-security-after-pupils-rescue/

item.title: Timaya Falls Off Stage During Live Performance in US
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Popular Nigerian musician Inetimi Timaya Odon, better known as Timaya, has gone viral online after falling off the stage during a live performance in Newark, United States.   The incident, which was captured in a video shared on the singer’s Instagram page, shows Timaya performing when a

item.title: Nigerian newspapers review: Gunmen raid Kogi school, abduct principal, NECO official, students
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: In today’s Nigerian newspapers review programme, Today in the News, Vanguard leads with gunmen raiding a school in Kogi State and abducting the principal, an official of the National Examination Council (NECO) and students. The post Nigerian newspapers review: Gunmen raid Kogi school, abduct princip

item.title: Ajayi Targets Second Diamond League Win In London
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Fresh from a stunning victory on his Diamond League debut, Nigerian record holder Kayinsola Ajayi returns to action at the London Diamond League on Saturday, aiming for a second consecutive 100m win against another world-class field. The 21-year-old heads to the British capital brimming with confide

item.title: Tuchel defends tactics after England World Cup semi final loss
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
England manager Thomas Tuchel defends his tactics after the Three Lions’ World Cup semi-final defeat to Argentina, despite facing scrutiny for his decision

item.title: Power Minister constitutes committee to tackle electricity market decentralisation issues
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Minister of Power, Joseph Tegbe, has constituted a nine-member inter-agency committee to drive continuous engagement among stakeholders, as well as to resolve emerging issues arising from the implementation of the Electricity Act, 2023. Tegbe, who would serve as chairman of the committee, while

item.title: Nigerians will eventually see full benefits of current reforms — Presidency
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Presidency has stated that Nigerians will soon begin to see the full benefits of the reforms embarked upon by President Bola Ahmed Tinubu‘s administration, stressing that the initial pains will give way to long-term gains. Mr. Tunde Rahman, Senior Special Assistant to the President on Medi

item.title: JAMB warns candidates against illegal admissions
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has warned candidates seeking admission into tertiary institutions against accepting admissions offered outside its Central Admissions Processing System (CAPS), saying such candidates risk losing recognition as legitimate students. The Board said a

item.title: Enekwechi, Onwuzurike Grab Confidence-Boosting Podiums In Budapest
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: African record holder Chukwuebuka Enekwechi and sprint star Udodi Onwuzurike gave Nigeria a timely lift ahead of the 2026 Commonwealth Games, both securing third-place finishes at the Gyulai István Memorial – Hungarian Athletics Grand Prix in Budapest. Enekwechi took bronze in the men’s shot p

item.title: UN can investigate school abductions in Nigeria, political scientist says
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Political scientist Wale Adewale has challenged Nigeria’s Permanent Representative to the United Nations, Ambassador Jimoh Ibrahim, over his claim that the UN lacks the authority to investigate the recent Oriire school abduction. In a statement, Adewale argued that the position taken by Ibrahim was

item.title: Tinubu, Shettima Re-nominations: Chief Tony Chukwu lauds APC, felicitates President, Vice President
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: By Tony Osagie A prominent political stakeholder in Imo State, Chief Tony Chukwu (Onwegi ihe kariri Chineke), has commended the All Progressives Congress (APC) for re-nominating President Bola Tinubu and Vice President Kashim Shettima as the 2027 Presidential and Vice Presidential candidates of the

item.title: CBN allots N1.19 trillion as investors bid N2.87 trillion for 364-day T-bill
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Investors in Nigeria’s Treasury Bills market poured a whopping N2.87 trillion into the 364-day instrument alone at the July 15 primary market auction, more than seven times higher than N400 billion on offer. The post CBN allots N1.19 trillion as investors bid N2.87 trillion for 364-day T-bill appear

item.title: WHO backs Nigeria’s push to curb drug misuse, expand medicine access
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The WHO supports Nigeria’s intensified efforts to curb drug misuse while ensuring access to essential medicines for pain relief and mental health.
Read More: https://punchng.com/who-backs-nigerias-push-to-curb-drug-misuse-expand-medicine-access/

item.title: Super Falcons Defender Signs New Three-Year Contract With AS Roma
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Super Falcons defender Shukurat Oladipo has signed a new three-year contract with Italian champions AS Roma. The Giallorossi confirmed Oladipo’s contract extension in a statement published on the club’s official website. Oladipo Committed Future To AS Roma “AS Roma are delighted to

item.title: Tax Revenue Hits N21.6trn In H1 As Reforms Boost Economy
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nigeria’s tax revenue surged to N21.6 trillion in the first half of 2026, representing a 49 per cent year-on-year increase from N14.27 trillion recorded in the same period of 2025, as fiscal reforms begin to yield results. Special Adviser to the President on Economic Affairs, Dr. Tope Fasua, disclos

item.title: Presidential Media Team, Renewed Hope Ambassadors Inspect Landmark Projects in Imo
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Presidential Communications Team and the Renewed Hope Ambassadors have inspected key federal and state government projects in Imo State as part of a national media tour aimed at showcasing infrastructure development across the South-East. In a statement by the Director of Media & Publicity,

item.title: Annual Projects Review: Sokoto Govt Applauds AGILE’s Impact,Rekindles Hope For Education
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Sokoto State Government has commended the state AGILE implementation team for excellent performance in the delivery of AGILE activities across the state. The State Commissioner for Basic and Secondary Education, Prof. Ahmed Ladan Ala, stated this while declaring open a three-day annual review wo

item.title: FMBN Finances 6,911 Housing Units In 2025, Seeks Stronger Partnerships To Tackle Housing Deficit
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The managing director and chief executive officer of the Federal Mortgage Bank of Nigeria (FMBN), Shehu Usman Osidi, has disclosed that the bank financed 6,911 housing units in 2025, representing a 300 per cent increase over the 2,165 units delivered in 2024, as it intensified efforts to bridge Nige

item.title: Food Service Industry Defies Economic Headwinds, Grows To $11.09bn
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nigeria’s foodservice industry has demonstrated remarkable resilience despite rising inflation, higher food prices and changing consumer spending patterns, growing into an estimated $11.09 billion market driven by digital payments, online food delivery and technology-enabled business operation

item.title: Nigerian Army begins process to update 2017 defence doctrine
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Nigerian Army, 6 Division Headquarters, Port Harcourt Barracks, Rivers State, on Tuesday, 14 July 2026, received a delegation from the Committee on Review of the Joint Defence Doctrine 2017, JDD, which is working to produce a revised doctrine that captures Nigeria’s shifting security landscape a

item.description: Vice President Kashim Shettima has urged officials of the National Hajj Commission of Nigeria (NAHCON) to treat the administration of Hajj as a sacred trust, warning that those entrusted with the welfare of Nigerian pilgrims carry enormous spiritual and moral responsibilities. Represented by former

item.title: Imo East: Negotiating from Position of Strength for Maduka

item.description: Amby Uneze writes on the need for Mbaise sub-zone to use its position of strength to support Sir Basil Maduka towards 2027 Imo East Senatorial seat. Politics is not merely

item.title: Oye: A Life of Impact and Service to Humanity
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: As immediate past National Chairman of the All Progressives Grand Alliance, Chief Ikechukwu Oye clocks 70 on July 18, Onyebuchi Ezigbo writes on his momentous contributions to politics and humanity.

item.title: Mary Habila’s death: Umahi’s team of lawyers petition police, insist on autopsy
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Minister of Works, Engr David Umahi, through his team of lawyers, has petitioned the Ebonyi State Commissioner of Police, urging that an autopsy on the late Mary Habila be conducted before her remains are released for burial. In a letter dated July 15, 2026, and acknowledged by the Ebonyi State Poli

item.title: ‘$7bn Leaves Africa Every Year As Patients Travel Abroad For Medical Treatment’
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: African governments have been urged to shift their healthcare priorities from constructing hospital buildings to investing in the training, retention and welfare of medical professionals, with a warning that failure to do so could leave the continent with modern facilities but too few specialists to

item.description: Argentina could face disciplinary action from FIFA after several of its players celebrated their World Cup semi-final victory over England by displaying a banner supporting the country's claim to the Falkland Islands. item.title: 2027: No hope for ADC, NDC – Fayose
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: “Take a look at what they are doing to the opposition: the African Democratic Congress and the Nigeria Democratic Congress. They have taken them to square zero,” Fayose said.

item.title: EFCC releases Peter Obi’s supporters after questioning
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The  Economic and Financial Crimes Commission, EFCC, has released two supporters of the Nigeria Democratic Congress, NDC, presidential candidate, Peter Obi, held in its custody. The two supporters, Randy Peter and Precious Oruche, popularly known as Mama Pee, who are activists, regained their f

item.title: Fire at orphanage in Algeria kills 11 and wounds 19
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: A fire at an orphanage outside of Algiers claimed the lives of at least 11 people and wounded 19, the country's civil defence announced on Thursday.

item.title: Paramount shareholder sues Ellison family, alleges corruption in Warner Bros. Discovery takeover
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: A Paramount Skydance shareholder has filed a lawsuit accusing the company's controlling shareholders, Larry Ellison and his son David Ellison, of engaging in corrupt practices to secure regulatory approval for Paramount's proposed $111 billion acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery. The post Paramoun

item.title: WHO highlights Nigeria’s new push to combat substance abuse
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The organisation also stressed that its role at the summit was to provide technical expertise to help policymakers strike the right balance between controlling drug misuse and ensuring patients continue to receive medicines that are legally prescribed.

item.title: Digital lenders top Kenya’s consumer complaint list despite reforms
item.dc:creator: TechCabal
item.description: The increase made digital lenders the largest source of consumer complaints in financial services, accounting for nearly two-thirds of the sector's grievances.

item.title: US, Iran trade fresh strikes as Hormuz conflict escalates

item.description:
The US and Iran exchange fresh strikes over the vital Strait of Hormuz, escalating the Middle East conflict. Learn about the latest developments in the US

item.title: Niger Assembly has no website. It’s limiting residents’ access to information
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Twenty-seven years after Nigeria returned to democratic rule in 1999, the Niger State House of Assembly still has no official website where citizens can access bills, committee reports, voting records and other legislative documents. The post Niger Assembly has no website. It’s limiting reside

item.title: Lagos Arrests 12 Environmental Offenders In Crackdown On Illegal Waste Disposal
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Lagos State Government has arrested 12 environmental offenders during enforcement operations across Lagos Island, Yaba and Ikorodu as part of intensified efforts to tackle indiscriminate waste disposal and other environmental violations. In a post on its official X page on Thursday, the state sa

item.title: Iran targets military bases as US launches wave of strikes
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Explosions were heard across Iran overnight, shortly after neighbouring Gulf states began to report attacks.

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Lionel Messi described Argentina's dramatic 2-1 victory over England in the World Cup semi-final as "special" after inspiring the defending champions to a remarkable comeback and a place in Sunday's final against Spain. The post Messi savours ‘special’ England victory as Argentina reach

item.title: First Securities Launches FirstInvest App
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: First Securities Brokers, a subsidiary of FirstHoldCo Plc, has announced the launch of the FirstInvest App, an innovative mobile investment platform designed to make stock market investing simpler, faster, and more accessible for Nigerians. The FirstInvest App offers investors a seamless digital exp

item.title: Police crack down on cultism in secondary school, recover firearm from student in Taraba
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Taraba State police command has arrested an 18 year old student suspected to be involved in cult-related activities and recovered an illegal firearm in Jalingo, the state capital. The arrest, according to the police, followed a report received by the GRA Division of the command on July 14, 2026,

item.title: Mary Habila’s death: Police insist on autopsy amid growing controversies

item.title: 93% of inmates in correctional centres nationwide are state offenders — FG
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Federal Government on Wednesday revealed that 93 per cent of inmates in correctional centres across Nigeria are state offenders, while only seven per cent are being held for federal offences, revealing that many of those in custody were incarcerated for minor offences that did not warrant impris

item.title: 79 percent of Nigerians face poverty despite Tinubu’s reforms – World Bank
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The World Bank has revealed that about 79 percent of Nigerians remain poor or vulnerable to falling into poverty despite nearly three years of sweeping economic reforms introduced by the administration of President Bola Tinubu. This is contained in the World Bank’s newly approved Country Partnership

item.title: World Cup: Messi dismisses claims of FIFA favouritism towards Argentina
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Argentina captain, Lionel Messi, has rejected claims that FIFA has shown favouritism towards his team following their 2-1 victory over England in the semi-final of the 2026 FIFA World Cup on Wednesday. The post World Cup: Messi dismisses claims of FIFA favouritism towards Argentina appeared first on

item.title: Oil Prices Rise As Hostilities Worsen In Middle East
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Oil extended gains as the U.S. said it had begun a new wave of strikes against Iranian military ‌installations on Wednesday, aiming to limit Tehran’s ability to strike commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. Washington had earlier reimposed a naval blockade of Iranian ports and launched o

item.title: MacIntyre holes monster putt for dream start at The Open!
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Robert MacIntyre made a birdie on the first hole at Royal Birkdale to get his Open underway in style.

item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Joint Admissions Matriculation Board (JAMB)’s Public Communication Advisor, Dr Fabian Benjamin says the Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME) fees may rise again due to economic pressure, defends one-year validity of results.. item.title: Soludo hails Tinubu over approval of strategic South-East roads
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Anambra State Governor, Chukwuma Soludo, has commended President Bola Tinubu for approving the design and procurement of two major highway projects, describing the move as a significant boost to infrastructure development in the South-East. The post Soludo hails Tinubu over approval of strategic Sou

item.title: Protests against removal of popular defence minister erupt across Ukraine
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Rare wartime protests in Ukraine erupted across the country on Thursday with demonstrators demanding that President Volodymyr Zelensky reappoint Mykhailo Fedorov, who was removed from office on Wednesday.

item.title: Romero labels Neville 'stupid' over criticism
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Argentina defender Cristian Romero says player-turned-pundit Gary Neville was "stupid" to criticise his centre-back partnership with Lisandro Martinez.

item.dc:creator: Techpoint Africa
item.description: Modern AI is built on one bet: train a model...

item.description: The Federal High Court in Abuja has ordered the final forfeiture of 48 properties linked to former Attorney-General of the Federation and Minister of Justice, Abubakar Malami (SAN), to the Federal Government, in what is one of the EFCC’s biggest non-conviction-based asset recovery cases. &#160

item.title: Power Minister Sets Up 9-Member Committee to Drive Electricity Market Decentralisation
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Minister of Power, Chief Joseph Olasunkanmi Tegbe, has inaugurated a nine-member inter-agency committee to address implementation issues surrounding Nigeria’s transition to a decentralised electricity market, describing the reform as one of the most significant changes in the country&#8217

item.title: INEC partners NIMC for transparent elections, clean voter register
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: INEC and NIMC have deepened collaboration to improve voter register integrity, reduce duplication and enhance transparency ahead of 2027 elections. The post INEC partners NIMC for transparent elections, clean voter register appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Death of Physiotherapist in Umahi’s Residence: Family rejects autopsy as police insist on post-mortem
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Jeff Agbodo, Abakaliki The Ebonyi State Police Command has insisted on conducting a post-mortem examination into the death of Miss Mary Habila, a physiotherapist who died at the residence of the Minister of Works, Senator David Umahi, in Uburu community of the state. The command said preliminary

item.title: US approves nearly $2bn in weapons sales to Saudi Arabia
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The move comes as Saudi Arabia appears to be on the brink of renewed war with Yemen's Iran-backed Houthis after they fired missiles at an airport in the southern Saudi city of Abha on Monday. The post US approves nearly $2bn in weapons sales to Saudi Arabia appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: 71 seafarers killed, 17 bulk carriers lost in 10 years — INTERCARGO
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: INTERCARGO, the global body of Dry cargo ship owners, have said between 2016 to 2025, 17 bulk carriers of 10,000 deadweight (dwt) about were lost, representing 1.63 million dwt, with 71 seafarers. The post 71 seafarers killed, 17 bulk carriers lost in 10 years — INTERCARGO appeared first on Tribune

item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: "One of the suspects was apprehended while escorting the consignment, while another suspected member of the syndicate was subsequently arrested during follow-on operations." item.title: Witch doctor made me kill my aunt, six children – Kano murder suspect
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The principal suspect in the killing of a woman and her six children in Kano has told the Kano State High Court that he carried out the attack because a witch doctor instructed him to do so. The suspect, 23-year-old Umar Auwalu, made the claim on Wednesday while giving evidence before Justice Nasiru

item.title: 2027: APC woos Kwankwaso ally as NDC cracks widen
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The APC leaders reportedly approached the senator after he failed to secure the NDC ticket to contest for another term in Kano Central.

item.title: EFCC releases activists Randy Peter, Mama Pee
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The EFCC has released activists Randy Peter and Mama Pee after hours of questioning in Abuja. They are expected to return to the commission on Thursday.
Read More: https://punchng.com/efcc-releases-activists-randy-peter-mama-pee/

item.title: South Korea to Halt New Listings of Single Stock Leveraged ETFs
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: South Korea will temporarily halt new listings of single-stock leveraged exchange traded products to curb market volatility after a surge in popularity of funds tied to Samsung Electronics Co. and SK Hynix Inc.

item.title: NIWA Moves To Sanitize Nigeria’s Waterways With Jetty, Boat Reforms — Shagari
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The National Inland Waterways Authority (NIWA) has unveiled an ambitious nationwide reform programme aimed at rehabilitating dilapidated jetties, phasing out unsafe boats, and accelerating the modernization of Nigeria’s inland water transport system through strategic partnerships with indigenous boa

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Argentina stars, Cristian Romero and Lisandro Martinez, have fired back at Manchester United legend Gary Neville after Lionel Scaloni’s side defeated England 2-1 in the World Cup semi-final on Wednesday. Neville had recently labelled Martinez and Romero as ‘the best worst centre-halves in

item.title: Tuchel defends tactics after England World Cup dream dies
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Tuchel is likely to face serious scrutiny for his substitutions, notably the decision to bring goalscorer Gordon off for defender Ezri Konsa in the 72nd minute. The post Tuchel defends tactics after England World Cup dream dies appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Messi said victory in the semi-final was "quite a special one, especially playing against England with all the historical context". The post Messi hails ‘special’ World Cup win over England appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Congo-Kinshasa: Scientists Confirm New Monkey Species in Congo
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [allAfrica] Scientists have discovered a new species of monkey, the Colobus congoensis, that has striking pinkish-orange lips and a black face and lives in the forests of the Democratic Republic of Congo.

item.description: Lionel Messi produced another unforgettable World Cup performance on Wednesday night but even his brilliance could not erase a long-standing football record held by Nigerian legend Jay-Jay Okocha, Soccernet.ng reports. The 39-year-old inspired Argentina to a dramatic 2-1 comeback victory over Englan

item.title: NASS to place state police on first-line charge, says Senate
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: LIKE the Judiciary, the National Assembly plans to place the proposed State Police Service on First-Line Charge in order to insulate its operations from undue influence by state governors. item.title: Boosting research, varsity ranking top RSU ‘Faculty Away Day’ by governing council
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Steps to move the Rivers State University (RSU), formerly University of Science and Technology (UST), to at least 3rd best read more Boosting research, varsity ranking top RSU ‘Faculty Away Day’ by governing council

item.title: Placenta: Where culture meets science
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: In many cultures, the placenta is more than tissue left over after birth. It is understood as spiritually linked to the baby, treated as a companion or sibling, and buried to protect the child or connect them to family and ancestral land. The post Placenta: Where culture meets science appeared first

item.title: ‘History will be kind to you’ — Soludo thanks Tinubu over Southeast road projects
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The governor said he received confirmation from the Ministry of Works on July 15 that the President had approved both roads for design and procurement. The post ‘History will be kind to you’ — Soludo thanks Tinubu over Southeast road projects appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: NIWA board chairman seeks global expertise for Nigeria’s brown waters
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: THE chairman of the Governing Board of the National Inland Waterways Authority (NIWA), Mukhtar Shehu Shagari, has intensified efforts to strengthen Nigeria’s inland waterways sector through international collaboration The post NIWA board chairman seeks global expertise for Nigeria’s brown waters app

item.title: Steiner Digs In at Ocado as Warehouse Tech Unit Falters
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Ocado Group Plc Chief Executive Officer Tim Steiner said he remains committed to leading the British grocery delivery company, as it announced another weak performance from its key technology division.

item.title: Nigeria’s foreign reserves hit $51.86 billion, highest since 2009
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Nigeria’s external reserves have climbed to $51.86 billion, marking their highest level in more than 17 years and surpassing the Central Bank of Nigeria’s (CBN) projection for the year. The post Nigeria’s foreign reserves hit $51.86 billion, highest since 2009 appeared first on Nairametrics.

item.title: Nigeria Emerges as Africa’s Regulatory Bright Spot as New Report Reveals $17.8bn Opportunity Lost to Unlicensed Gambling
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Nigeria’s online gaming industry may be closer than any other major African market to solving one of the continent’s biggest read more Nigeria Emerges as Africa’s Regulatory Bright Spot as New Report Reveals $17.8bn Opportunity Lost to Unlicensed Gambling

item.title: Asia Stocks Tumble On Tech Worries, Mideast War
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Japan's Nikkei 225 was 3.16 per cent lower at 66,579.31 points, while the Kospi in South Korea was down 6.21 per cent at 6,831.80. The post Asia Stocks Tumble On Tech Worries, Mideast War appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Rwanda launches eKash instant payments platform
item.dc:creator: Techpoint Africa
item.description: On Techpoint Digest, we discuss telecoms in Niger facing new regulatory scrutiny, Rwanda launching the eKash instant payment platform, and IHS Towers securing new funding for growth.

item.title: Global PR Day: Meet 10 Nigerian women shaping the future of public relations in 2026
item.dc:creator: Technext
item.description: Every public relations professional knows the feeling of having to explain what they actually do. And for many…

item.title: How hard is it for Littler to go back to back at the World Matchplay?
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: The World Matchplay was a tournament dominated by Phil Taylor as - from 1994 to 2014 - he picked up 15 wins in Blackpool out of a possible 21. Yes, you read that correctly.

item.title: South Africa: 10 things about Tony Leon, the politician at the centre of a lobbying row
item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: Once the face of South Africa’s opposition, Tony Leon is back in the spotlight after his communications firm was accused of using his political connections to influence DA ministers, lawmakers and municipalities, allegations he denies.

item.title: Nigeria’s credit rating upgrade is welcome. The journey to an ‘A’ has only just begun
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Standard & Poor’s decision to upgrade Nigeria’s sovereign credit rating from B- to B, with a stable outlook, marks one read more Nigeria’s credit rating upgrade is welcome. The journey to an ‘A’ has only just begun

item.title: 2027: Atiku’s supporters fighting Obi, not Tinubu – Galadima
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: “Unfortunately, it’s not only the government of APC. Even some opposition political parties, instead of us fighting our common opponent, that is the APC and its government, we are fighting ourselves.”
